habitat: Steep; rocky terrain in forests; open habitats at 1000–4400 m; coniferous and mixed forests
story: Explore the fascinating world of the Chinese Goral (Naemorhedus griseus). Classified under the Bovidae family, this bird predominantly inhabits Steep; rocky terrain in forests; open habitats at 1000–4400 m; coniferous and mixed forests. The current IUCN classification of Vulnerable (VU) underscores the importance of safeguarding its natural range. From its feeding habits to its nesting preferences, the Chinese Goral is a testament to the intricate web of life. By studying species like the Chinese Goral, we gain valuable insights into broader ecological trends.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
